name: Probe
description: Local semantic + lexical code search built for AI agents; no cloud, AST-aware

developer-org.comment: Open-source project under the probelabs GitHub org.

implementation-language.comment: Search engine core is Rust (~73%); the CLI, MCP server, and agent SDK layer are JavaScript/Node (~26%), per GitHub language breakdown 2026-07-09.

first-release.comment: GitHub repo created 2025-03-05; no earlier public announcement found, so month-of-repo-creation used as first-release proxy.

status.comment: Latest release v0.6.0-rc327 on 2026-06-27; actively developed (pre-1.0, still on release candidates).

form-factor.comment: Ships as a CLI, an MCP server (agent and raw-tools modes), and a Node.js SDK/library exposing search()/extract()/query() and a ProbeAgent class.

standalone-usable.comment: Pluggable into any MCP-capable agent or used directly as a CLI/library; not welded to a single host.

bundled-host.comment: Standalone tool, not a bundled engine.

install-mechanism.comment: Primary install via npm; curl (macOS/Linux) / PowerShell (Windows) install scripts fetch a prebuilt binary; cargo build from source also supported.

retrieval-technique.comment: Fuses ripgrep-speed lexical search (Elasticsearch-style boolean queries + BM25/TF-IDF) with tree-sitter AST parsing in a single retrieval path to return complete code blocks — hybrid tagged for that lexical+AST fusion. No embeddings/vector search ('zero indexing, no embedding models, no vector databases').

query-interface.comment: Core query is Elasticsearch-style boolean/lexical syntax (AND/OR/NOT, exact phrases, ext:/file:/dir: filters, plus AST metavariable patterns). Exposed as MCP tool calls; the built-in Probe Agent accepts natural-language questions.

returns.comment: Returns complete functions, classes, or structs (whole syntax blocks) rather than fixed-size text windows.

structural-awareness.comment: Uses tree-sitter AST parsing to understand code structure and return complete syntax nodes.

chunking-strategy.comment: Extraction boundaries follow AST syntax nodes; results are whole functions/classes/structs (symbol-level).

reranking.comment: Configurable ranking (bm25/tfidf/hybrid/hybrid2) plus an optional second-stage BERT reranker via --reranker.

index-location.comment: Zero indexing — searches on-the-fly like ripgrep; nothing persistent is built.

incremental-updates.comment: No persistent index, so incremental re-indexing does not apply; every query searches the live tree directly.

real-time-watch.comment: No index/watcher; on-the-fly search always reads current files, so there is nothing to auto-update.

vector-store.comment: No embeddings, so no vector store.

embedding-model.comment: n-a — non-embedding technique (lexical + AST). Optional BERT model is used only for reranking, not embedding retrieval.

mcp-support.comment: Ships an MCP server (agent mode and raw-tools mode).

agent-compatibility.comment: Documented integrations with Cline, Roo, Cursor, Windsurf, or any MCP-compatible AI assistant.

ide-integrations.comment: CLI runs in the terminal; surfaces in VS Code-based editors via MCP-capable extensions (Cline, Roo, Cursor, Windsurf).

language-agnostic.comment: AST-aware block extraction depends on tree-sitter parser support for the listed languages; ripgrep-style text search works on any file, but the structural retrieval that defines the tool is language-limited.

supported-languages.value: Rust, JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, C/C++, Java, Ruby, PHP, Swift, Solidity, Crystal, C#, Markdown (tree-sitter parsers)

code-leaves-machine.comment: Search is fully local — 'your code never leaves your machine, no API calls for search.' (The optional Probe Agent mode calls an external LLM, but the search/extract engine itself is offline.)

local-only-option.comment: Core search runs fully offline with no external API and no embedding service.

self-hostable.comment: OSS tool that runs entirely on the user's machine.

telemetry.comment: No telemetry documented for this local OSS tool.

max-repo-scale.comment: Positioned for enterprise-scale codebases; ripgrep-based on-the-fly search scales to large repos. No hard size limit stated (marketing claim, not benchmarked).

maturity.comment: Usable but evolving — still pre-1.0 (v0.6.0 release candidates), ~659 stars, single-org project as of 2026-07-09.

active-maintenance.comment: Repo pushed 2026-06-27 and release v0.6.0-rc327 dated 2026-06-27, well within 90 days of the 2026-07-09 research date.

community-size.comment: ~659 GitHub stars (2026-07-09), single-org project — small relative to major tools in this space.
